assumptions: Pass 1 contract and route-surface edits are treated as already applied.
This pass does not modify implementation code.
This artifact is a planning and reconciliation map for later targeted Codex write packages.
source_authority: Vouch legal and product source documents

notes: Pass 2 intentionally records drift instead of fixing it.
Route-level forbidden surfaces are assumed handled by Pass 1.
The largest remaining drift appears to be non-route settings/setup/admin implementation surfaces and legacy state vocabulary.

findings.source_rule: Vouch does not expose internal setup/settings pages. Connect and Payment are external Stripe flows, not internal Vouch pages.
findings.repo_evidence: Settings-oriented modules remain outside the route layer.
findings.risk: Future implementation work may accidentally preserve settings as a product surface instead of folding readiness and provider redirects into canonical dashboard/create/detail flows.
reconciliation_plan: Do not delete blindly in Pass 2.
Inventory all imports of settings modules in Pass 5 and Pass 8.
Move reusable readiness display into canonical flow components only if still needed.
Rename payment/payout setup actions to Stripe redirect/readiness actions where retained.
Remove settings page features once their useful logic is migrated.

findings.source_rule: Setup/readiness logic is allowed as internal gating, but setup must not become a standalone product route or dashboard surface.
findings.repo_evidence: Setup-named types, library logic, actions, and tests remain.
findings.risk: Useful readiness logic may be coupled to obsolete setup-page assumptions.
reconciliation_plan: Retain readiness gate logic only if it supports create, accept, payment redirect, payout redirect, or confirmation flows.
Rename setup-facing concepts to readiness or account-gate concepts where appropriate.
Remove user-facing setup page assumptions.
Update tests to validate readiness as embedded flow gating, not a setup page.

findings.source_rule: Vouch has no admin arbitration, manual settlement, dispute, appeal, evidence, or user-facing admin settlement surface. Internal safe retry/inspection may exist only if non-discretionary.
findings.repo_evidence: Admin feature, action, fetcher, schema, type, authz, and navigation surfaces remain.
findings.risk: Admin modules may encode support override, manual settlement, or arbitration-style behavior if not constrained.
reconciliation_plan: Audit admin modules for manual payout, force release, manual refund decision, confirmation rewrite, settlement override, evidence, dispute, and appeal semantics.
Delete user-facing admin feature pages and navigation if Pass 1 did not remove them.
Retain only server-only operational retry/inspection primitives that are deterministic and provider-aware.
Move retained internal operational logic under system/recovery naming rather than admin product naming.

findings.source_rule: Canonical Vouch lifecycle is draft, committed, sent, accepted, authorized, confirmable, completed, expired. Payment/provider/settlement/archive/recovery states are separate axes.
findings.repo_evidence: VouchStatus and status-related code are spread across Prisma, types, schemas, state helpers, resolution logic, transactions, fetchers, components, and tests.
findings.risk: Old statuses such as pending, active, refunded, canceled, failed, voided, provider_restricted, capture_failed, or refund_failed may still pollute VouchStatus.
reconciliation_plan: Pass 3 must inspect schema enum values directly.
Pass 4 must normalize constants, TypeScript unions, and Zod schemas.
Pass 6 must update transactions/actions to use canonical lifecycle transitions.
Pass 8 must update status badge display after canonical state normalization.

findings.source_rule: Canonical confirmation methods are code_exchange and offline_code_exchange. GPS, manual, system, screenshots, evidence, and support confirmation are forbidden.
findings.repo_evidence: Confirmation method/state code requires deeper inspection across schema, types, schemas, transactions, and resolution.
findings.risk: Old manual/gps/system confirmation methods may allow noncanonical settlement paths.
reconciliation_plan: Pass 3 must inspect Prisma ConfirmationMethod.
Pass 4 must normalize ConfirmationMethod constants/types/schemas.
Pass 6 must ensure transactions reject duplicate, early, late, manual, GPS, and support confirmations.
Pass 7 must ensure settlement only follows bilateral verified confirmation.

findings.source_rule: Canonical roles are merchant and customer. Payer/payee may exist only as documented payment aliases.
findings.repo_evidence: Role-related code exists across Vouch types, authz, selects, transactions, fetchers, and Prisma.
findings.risk: Independent payer/payee and merchant/customer naming may cause role confusion in authorization and settlement.
reconciliation_plan: Map merchant to payee and customer to payer only inside payment-specific code if needed.
Use merchant/customer in product-facing DTOs, schemas, UI, and authorization.
Document aliases centrally if retained.

findings.source_rule: All protected reads go through fetchers. All writes go through server actions. Actions must authenticate, authorize, validate, mutate/provider-call, audit, and revalidate.
findings.repo_evidence: Action and fetcher surfaces exist, but Pass 2 does not inspect internal sequence correctness.
findings.risk: Actions may skip authz, Zod validation, audit, or provider-state retrieval if not audited function-by-function.
reconciliation_plan: Pass 6 must inspect server action sequencing.
Pass 5 must inspect fetcher authentication, authorization, select, DTO mapping, and cache policy.
Retain notification and analytics only if they remain system-generated and non-messaging/non-dispute.

findings.source_rule: Selects must be minimal, DTO-oriented, transport-safe, and must not be driven by removed setup/settings/admin surfaces.
findings.repo_evidence: Selects exist for settings and core domains.
findings.risk: Settings/admin select shapes may overfetch or preserve obsolete product assumptions.
reconciliation_plan: Pass 5 must remove or reframe settings selects.
Pass 5 must ensure dashboard card select is smaller than detail select.
Pass 5 must ensure participant DTOs do not receive admin/system-only data.

findings.source_rule: Stripe owns payment truth, Vouch owns workflow truth, and webhooks reconcile provider truth idempotently.
findings.repo_evidence: Stripe and provider surfaces exist, but Pass 2 does not inspect retrieve-before-settlement or webhook idempotency internals.
findings.risk: Payment readiness and payout readiness may still be coupled to settings/setup screens or browser return truth.
reconciliation_plan: Pass 7 must inspect manual-capture PaymentIntent creation.
Pass 7 must verify retrieve-before-capture/cancel/refund.
Pass 7 must verify webhook event ID dedupe and late-event reconciliation.
Pass 7 must ensure Stripe return pages do not finalize provider truth.

findings.source_rule: Tenant navigation contains only Dashboard, Vouches, Connect, and Payment. Connect and Payment trigger server actions to Stripe-hosted flows.
findings.repo_evidence: Navigation components and route protection proxy exist.
findings.risk: Navigation may still expose settings/setup/admin or internal payment/payout pages.
reconciliation_plan: Pass 8 must inspect all navigation components.
Pass 6 must ensure Connect and Payment navigation invokes server actions rather than internal pages.
Pass 9 must include route-surface e2e checks.

findings.source_rule: Analytics and notifications are allowed only as operational/product instrumentation and system-generated notices, not messaging, negotiation, dispute, or evidence channels.
findings.risk: Notifications could drift into messaging or negotiation if not constrained.
reconciliation_plan: Pass 6 and Pass 8 must verify notification events are system-generated only.
Pass 5 must ensure analytics DTOs do not expose sensitive provider, identity, dispute, or meeting-purpose data.

findings.source_rule: Generated Prisma artifacts should not be manually edited. They should change only through schema normalization and Prisma generate.
findings.repo_evidence: Generated Prisma files appear in search results for state and admin terms.
findings.risk: Codex may attempt to edit generated files directly.
reconciliation_plan: Never edit prisma/generated files by hand.
Pass 3 should edit prisma/schema.prisma only.
Run pnpm prisma:generate after schema changes if required.

findings.source_rule: Tests must enforce source-of-truth constraints instead of preserving obsolete setup/settings/admin/lifecycle behavior.
findings.repo_evidence: Route surface, setup, status, status badge, and Stripe tests exist.
findings.risk: Tests may keep obsolete concepts alive after implementation is corrected.
reconciliation_plan: Pass 9 must update tests after state, action, Stripe, and UI corrections.
Do not weaken tests to pass old behavior.
Convert setup tests into readiness-gate tests if the logic remains valid.

findings.source_rule: Agent instructions, docs, contracts, and execution artifacts must reinforce the current source of truth.
findings.repo_evidence: Agent docs and execution files exist and should remain synchronized with the audit program.
findings.risk: Execution artifacts may point Codex back toward obsolete setup/settings/admin work.
reconciliation_plan: Pass 9 must reconcile execution JSON after implementation corrections pass validation.
Do not edit broad docs in Pass 2 except this audit artifact.

findings.source_rule: Validation should catch contract drift and forbidden surface reintroduction.
findings.repo_evidence: validate-contracts.mjs validates parseability and required file presence but does not enforce semantic route/source conformance.
findings.risk: Future drift can re-enter unless semantic checks live in tests or validation scripts.
reconciliation_plan: Pass 9 should decide whether to add a dedicated source-conformance validation script.
For now, keep semantic enforcement in tests/contract/architecture-surface.test.ts and future contract tests.

findings.source_rule: Vouch must not include disputes, mediation, arbitration, appeals, evidence review, screenshots, marketplace, messaging, reviews, ratings, browse, search, providers, public profiles, or service listings.
findings.repo_evidence: Initial broad search did not surface obvious dispute/evidence/marketplace/review/messaging files, but targeted searches should be repeated locally with rg.
findings.risk: Terms may exist inside copy or comments that GitHub search did not surface.
reconciliation_plan: Run local ripgrep during each implementation pass.
Treat any surfaced forbidden term as remove, rename/reframe, or internal-only with justification.

findings.source_rule: Contracts and route surface first; then schema/state; then constants/types/schemas; then reads/writes; then integrations; then UI/tests.
findings.repo_evidence: Pass 2 findings confirm later passes should not start with UI.
findings.risk: Starting with UI would preserve wrong state, role, readiness, and admin assumptions.
reconciliation_plan: Proceed to Pass 3: Prisma schema and state-axis normalization.
Do not use Codex for broad UI edits before schema/state contracts are normalized.

next_pass.name: Prisma schema and state-axis normalization
primary_targets: generated Prisma files only through prisma generate
state-axis impact notes for later constants/types/schemas
